title: Umami Polish Tempeh Wrap
description: A chewy, fermented tempeh wrap with plum tomatoes, fresh basil, and a flambeed soy glaze in a corn‑husk vessel.
instructions: Slice the tempeh into 1/2 cm thick strips.
Marinate the tempeh in soy sauce, mirin, sesame oil, salt, and pepper for 10 minutes.
Heat a non-stick skillet over medium-high heat and sear the tempeh for 3 minutes on each side until golden brown.
Add 30ml of white wine to the pan, allow it to flame, and let it reduce for 1 minute to create a flambeed glaze.
Remove the pan from heat and set the tempeh aside to rest.
Sauté the sliced plum tomatoes in a splash of olive oil for 2 minutes until just softened.
Lay a corn husk wrap flat, spread a thin layer of horseradish sauce, then arrange the glazed tempeh, sautéed tomatoes, and fresh basil across the center.
Fold the sides of the wrap inward and roll tightly, securing with a toothpick if desired, then serve immediately.
